title: Request Targets, Host Authority, Client Identity, and Proxy Trust · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed operations handoff reconstructs request targets, host authority, client identity, and proxy trust from bounded request and server evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the request authority and trusted-proxy decision record. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for request authority and trusted-proxy decision record after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence d…
objectives: Interpret Method, URL, Host, RequestURI, Header, Body, Trailer, RemoteAddr, TLS, Pattern, and Context using server-side semantics.
Validate authoritative host and port against a configured allowlist before routing, link construction, cache keys, or tenant selection.
Accept Forwarded or X-Forwarded values only from configured proxy hops and derive client, scheme, and host with a documented traversal rule.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Separate evidence from confidence · Review Request Identity Redaction
steps.instruction:
review case — A review team handles case 6042 in a public status API serving assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive effective authority only through an explicit trusted-proxy boundary; then change the tenant and show deny-by-default before repository access. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Record low-cardinality route, request ID, trusted peer, and redacted target evidence without logging credentials, tokens, or personal data.

Inspect the Request Identity Redaction trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Go server Separate evidence from confidence · Review Request Identity Redaction.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for record low-cardinality route, request id, trusted peer, and redacted target evidence without logging credentials, tokens, or personal data. For Go server Separate evidence from confidence · Review Request Identity Redaction, keep Request Identity Redaction connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
